privacy.intro: ReKindle est une application web conçue pour fournir un tableau de bord pour les appareils E-ink. Nous accordons la priorité à votre vie privée et à la transparence. Le client Telegram se connecte directement à une API Beeper Desktop configurée par l'utilisateur ; l'adresse et le jeton d'accès ne sont enregistrés que dans ce navigateur si l'utilisateur choisit de les mémoriser.
privacy.section.1.body:
ReKindle fonctionne selon deux modes :

  • Mode Invité : Aucune donnée n'est stockée de façon permanente sur nos serveurs. Toutes les données d'application sont stockées localement dans le localStorage de votre navigateur.
  • Mode Connecté : Vos données sont synchronisées de manière sécurisée avec Google Firebase sous votre ID utilisateur unique afin de fournir une expérience cohérente sur tous vos appareils.
privacy.section.1.title: 1. Architecture des Données
privacy.section.2.body:
ReKindle propose une intégration optionnelle avec les services Google pour afficher vos données personnelles sur votre tableau de bord.

  • Données consultées : Listes de tâches, événements de calendrier et carnet d'adresses.
  • Utilisation : Ces données sont récupérées uniquement pour être affichées au sein de l'application ReKindle.
  • Authentification : Nous utilisons Google OAuth 2.0 pour un accès sécurisé. Vous pouvez révoquer l'accès à tout moment via les paramètres de sécurité de votre compte Google.
  • Stockage : Vos données de Tâches, Calendrier et Contacts Google ne sont pas stockées sur les serveurs de ReKindle ou Firebase. Elles sont récupérées directement depuis les API Google par votre navigateur.

Update (June 2026): We load the Google APIs Client Library (apis.google.com) for OAuth and service discovery. No tracking cookies are placed.
privacy.section.2.title: 2. Données Utilisateur Google (Tâches, Calendrier, Contacts)
privacy.section.3.body:
Nous employons des mesures de sécurité rigoureuses pour protéger vos informations :

  • Chiffrement en Transit : Toutes les données transmises entre votre appareil et nos services sont protégées par le chiffrement TLS/SSL.
  • Chiffrement au Repos : Les données sensibles stockées dans notre base de données cloud (Firebase) sont protégées par le chiffrement AES-256.
  • Données Sensibles Chiffrées : Les identifiants tiers, tels que les mots de passe IMAP/SMTP et les SID Substack, sont chiffrés avec la clé de votre compte avant d'être envoyés dans le cloud, garantissant que vous seul pouvez y accéder.
privacy.section.3.title: 3. Mesures de Protection des Données
privacy.section.4.body:
If you create a ReKindle account, supported application data is stored in the primary Google Firebase project (rekindle-fork) under your user ID to enable synchronization across devices:

  • Tasks & Quick ToDo: To-do items created in the main Tasks app or generated via handwriting in Quick ToDo.
  • Calendar (Native): Events added to the native calendar if Google Calendar is not enabled.
  • Contacts (Native): Contacts added manually to the native address book if Google Contacts is not enabled.
  • Notes: The text content, formatting, timestamps, and sorting preferences of your notes.
  • Library: Your library index, reading progress (CFI position), daily reading duration, and personal book reviews/ratings. The actual book files are stored locally in your browser cache (IndexedDB) to save bandwidth, but the metadata is synced.
  • Settings: General preferences, wallpaper selection, integration toggles, display mode, auto-scaling, font preferences (including OpenDyslexic), unit system, and your custom AI API keys or third-party tokens (stored in owner-only private settings when sync is enabled).
  • Mail (IMAP/SMTP): Your email account configuration (server addresses, ports, username, and password). Passwords are encrypted using AES-256 before storage. Email content is fetched directly from your mail server and is not stored on Firebase.
  • Clock: Your saved cities, alarms, and home location for the World Clock.
  • Crossword & Mini Crossword: Current puzzle state and grid progress.
  • Sudoku: Current puzzle state and pencil marks.
  • Nonograms (Picross): Puzzle progress and solved states.
  • Minesweeper: Current game state and timer.
  • Nerdle: Daily game state and guesses.
  • Chess & Checkers: Win/Loss/Draw statistics.
  • Leaderboards: High scores for Tetris, Snake, Trivia, Tower of Hanoi, Lights Out, Mario, Dino, Pet, Block Blast, Wordle, Spelling Bee, Perfect Circle, Maze, and Codebreaker are stored publicly linked to your username.
  • Interactive Reader: Your library of saved text adventures and reading progress.
  • Chords: Your saved songs and chord sheets.
  • Trivia: Your personal game history and statistics.
  • Habit Tracker: Your habits and completion history.
  • Stocks: The list of stock ticker symbols you are watching.
  • Cookbook: Recipes you save.
  • RSS Reader: Your subscribed feed URLs.
  • Substack: Your Substack session ID (SID) is encrypted using AES-256 before storage to enable access to your paid subscriptions.
  • Reddit: Your list of saved subreddits.
  • Flashcards: Your decks, cards, and study intervals.
  • Countdown: Your saved event timers.
  • Spelling Bee: Your daily found words and puzzle statistics.
  • Words (Scrabble): Multiplayer game state and turn history.
  • Freewrite: Active writing sessions and saved documents.
  • Mindmap: Saved mindmaps and layouts.
  • Suggestions: Your submitted suggestions, problems, upvotes, and comments.
  • Watchlist: Your saved movies and TV shows.
  • AirType: Your saved freewrite notes and writing sessions.
  • Pixel Art: Your saved drawings, canvas data, and manifest library.
  • Sheet Music: Your personal library of scores. (Note: Scores you explicitly upload to the 'Online Store' are public).
  • Flipbook: Your animations and drawing manifests are synced to enable cross-device editing.
  • Food Log: Your categorized meals and daily food notes.
  • Journal: Your daily journal entries and reflections.
  • Live Multiplayer Games: Active game session state is stored in the Realtime Database for Chess, Checkers, Connect 4, Dots & Boxes, Pictionary, Tic-Tac-Toe, Uno, and Yahtzee.
  • Files & Docs: Your uploaded file metadata and text document drafts.
  • Teleprompter: Your saved speech scripts and scrolling preferences.
  • Spellbound: Your puzzle progress and stats.
  • Solitaire: Your saved game state and statistics.
  • Social Client Configs: Settings and access preferences for connected social platforms like Mastodon and Bluesky are securely stored if you authorize them.
  • Read Later: Your saved articles and reading list.
  • Life Calendar: Your life event entries and visual timeline data.
  • Account Credentials: Your synthetic email address ({username}@rekindle.ink) and hashed password for authentication. You may change your password at any time.
  • Dashboard: Your favorite apps layout and homepage preferences.
  • Books: Your reading log, reading duration, and personal reviews.
  • Weather: Your saved locations and weather display preferences.
  • Chat (AI Assistant): Your AI chat presets and conversation preferences.
  • ePub Reader: Your ePub reader settings and preferences.
  • Pet: Your virtual pet state and progression.
  • Pool: Your single-player billiards game state.
  • Dropbox: Your Dropbox OAuth token and synced file metadata.
  • Security & Abuse Prevention: Your IP address is securely logged when registering and logging in. This is used strictly to enforce bans against serial offenders and prevent the creation of multiple accounts by abusive users. It is not used for external tracking or advertising.
privacy.section.4.title: 4. Données de Synchronisation Cloud (Firebase)
privacy.section.5.body:
The following apps store data only on your specific device via browser localStorage (or are purely ephemeral), regardless of login status:

  • Browser: Your browsing history.
  • Language: Your completed units, lesson progress, and vocabulary stats.
  • Decide: Your saved decision options and lists.
  • Sketchpad (Napkin): Drawings are not saved to any persistent storage and are cleared upon exit or refresh.
  • ePub Reader: The ePub app is a tool for adding books to your main Reader library. It does not store data itself.
  • E-ink Sites: This app is a curated list of links and does not store any user data.
  • Pomodoro Timer: Session settings are ephemeral and not saved.
  • Calculator & Converter: No data is stored.
  • Breathing: Session settings are ephemeral.
  • Single Player Games: 2048 (High Score & Game State), Wordle (Daily Progress), Jigsaw, Memory, Solitaire, Connections (Bindings), Word Search, Anagrams, Hangman, Blackjack (Chip Balance), Pool (8-Ball), Codebreaker, Doom, Yahtzee, Oregon Trail, Strands, and Perfect Circle (personal high score).
  • Pass-and-Play Games: Game State for 2-Player Battleship, Chess, Checkers, Connect 4, Tic-Tac-Toe, Texas Hold'em, and Pool is not synced to the cloud.
  • Freewrite: Host/Client connection codes are ephemeral and not stored permanently.
  • Scriptures: Reading progress, bookmarks, and selected translations.
  • Translate: Your recent translation history and target languages.
  • Manga: Your reading progress and bookmarked series.
  • Libby: Your linked library cards are stored locally; book content is fetched securely via Libby's interface.
  • Readwise: Your API token and saved highlights are stored locally.
  • Pinterest: Your OAuth tokens and search history are stored locally.
  • Photo Frame: Your display mode, interval, and layout preferences are stored locally. Uploaded photos are stored in Firebase Storage (see Section 4).
  • Stopwatch: Session state is stored in localStorage as part of the Timer app.
  • Newspaper: Your font size preference.
  • Scores (Sports): Your favorite teams and leagues for the Sports app.
privacy.section.5.title: 5. Données Uniquement Locales
privacy.section.6.body:
ReKindle connects to the following third-party public APIs and services to provide functionality:

  • Google Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, and other LLMs: Used for the AI Assistant (Chat) intelligence. Your prompts are sent to these services via our secure proxy. Additionally, you may configure direct client-side connections to Groq, xAI (Grok), Mistral, Perplexity, Together AI, Fireworks AI, and local Ollama instances. These direct connections are user-configured and not proxied through our servers.
  • Yandex Cloud: API Gateway and Cloud Functions process authenticated AI, OCR, storage, mail, billing, Suggestions reports, and third-party proxy requests. Interactive story files and state are stored in Yandex Object Storage.
  • Gutendex: Used by the Reader app to search for and download public domain ebooks.
  • Standard Ebooks: Used by the Standard Ebooks app to fetch RSS feeds.
  • Open Library: Used for book search and cover images.
  • Google Books API: Used for book search.
  • DictionaryAPI & Wiktionary: Used by the Dictionary app for definitions.
  • Open-Meteo: Used for weather data and alerts.
  • Yahoo Finance: Used to fetch stock quotes.
  • CoinCap: Used to fetch cryptocurrency prices.
  • OpenStreetMap & Stadia Maps: Used by the Maps app for tiles, geocoding, and routing.
  • Reddit: Used to fetch public posts and comments.
  • Substack: Used to fetch public newsletter content and articles.
  • Dropbox: Used to sync files if you connect your Dropbox account.
  • Wikipedia & Wiktionary: Used to fetch daily history events and encyclopedia articles.
  • GitHub: Used as data sources for Crossword and Spelling Bee.
  • rss2json: Used by the Newspaper and RSS Reader apps.
  • Open Trivia Database: Used by the Trivia app for questions.
  • CORS Proxies: Various CORS proxy services are used to fetch content from external sources that don't support cross-origin requests.
  • Stripe: Used to securely process payments for ReKindle+.
  • Beeper Desktop API: Used by the Beeper Connect app to interface directly with your local Beeper instance over your local network.
  • Mastodon & Bluesky APIs: Used by their respective client apps to view your social timelines and post updates directly to those federated networks.
  • Lingva.ml, LibreTranslate & Tatoeba: Used by the Translate app to process your text queries.
  • MangaDex / Comic APIs: Used by the Manga app to fetch public comic chapters.
  • Unsplash / Display APIs: Used by the Photoframe app to display randomized background images.
  • Readwise API: Used to fetch your saved highlights and reading list.
  • Pinterest API: Used to fetch your saved pins and boards.
  • Bible API: Used by the Scriptures app to fetch religious texts.
  • Quran API: Used by the Scriptures app to fetch Quran texts.
  • TMDB (The Movie Database): Used by the Watchlist app for movie/TV search and metadata.
  • ESPN: Used by the Scores app to fetch live sports scores.
  • TheMealDB: Used by the Cookbook app for recipe search.
  • IFDB & IFArchive: Used by the Interactive Reader app for interactive fiction catalog and downloads.
  • GuitareTab: Used by the Chords app for guitar tab search.
  • DuckDuckGo HTML: Used by the Yandex Reader service for article search.
  • NY Times: Used by the Wordle and Strands apps for daily puzzle data.
  • Iconify: Used for icon search and display.
  • QR Server: Used by the AirType app to generate QR codes.
  • Ko-fi: Used to display the donation leaderboard on the Supporters page.
  • IPify: Used to fetch your public IP address for IP ban enforcement checks.
  • Open Exchange Rates: Used by the Converter app for currency conversion rates.
  • Counter.dev: Used for anonymous page-view analytics across the site.

CDN Libraries: We load open-source JavaScript libraries from public CDNs including cdn.jsdelivr.net, cdnjs.cloudflare.com, unpkg.com, and gstatic.com. These libraries include Firebase SDKs, marked.js, chess.js, Dropbox SDK, localForage, JSZip, EPUB.js, Mammoth, docx, FileSaver, QRCode, html5-qrcode, gif.js, OpenSheetMusicDisplay, and Tone.js MIDI. These CDNs may log your IP address per their own policies.
privacy.section.6.title: 6. Services Tiers
privacy.section.7.body:
Si vous avez des questions concernant cette politique :

Contact : contact@ukiyomusic.com
